source_pr.title: br: fix pitr with filter drop db issue and adjust tests
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #62486 #62528

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

Add a isRefreshMeta field in DDL schema diff. For table drop, if initiated by refreshMeta, don't check TiKV for old tableInfo as it doesn't make sense to BR case, and it will error out due to DB key not existed.
 
Add table filter to `br_pitr` int test
remove a few tests from `br_table_filter` as it has been tested in the comprehensive DDL tests. Removing those to reduce the test running time on CI
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

test.name: br: fix pitr with filter drop db issue and adjust tests

symptoms: run_sql "select count(*) DELETE_RANGE_CNT from (select * from mysql.gc_delete_range union all select * from mysql.gc_delete_range_done) del_range group by ts order by DELETE_RANGE_CNT desc limit 1;" (br/tests/br_pitr/run.sh)
failure_signature: run_sql "select count(*) DELETE_RANGE_CNT from (select * from mysql.gc_delete_range union all select * from mysql.gc_delete_range_done) del_range group by ts order by DELETE_RANGE_CNT desc limit 1;" (br/tests/br_pitr/run.sh)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it relied on nondeterministic result ordering (e.g., missing ORDER BY / unsorted assertions). Result ordering is not guaranteed unless explicitly enforced. This PR stabilizes it by: Add a isRefreshMeta field in DDL schema diff.
fix_pattern: Add a isRefreshMeta field in DDL schema diff. For table drop, if initiated by refreshMeta, don't check TiKV for old tableInfo as it doesn't make sense to BR case, and it will error out due to DB key not existed.

evidence: evidence: run_sql "select count(*) DELETE_RANGE_CNT from (select * from mysql.gc_delete_range union all select * from mysql.gc_delete_range_done) del_range group by ts order by DELETE_RANGE_CNT desc limit 1;" (br/tests/br_pitr/run.sh)
files: br/pkg/task/restore.go, br/tests/br_pitr/run.sh, br/tests/br_pitr_table_filter/run.sh, pkg/infoschema/builder.go, pkg/meta/model/job.go
